name: Cerro Capurutxo - Circular from La Font de la Figuera
name_original: El Cerro Capurutxo — Circular desde La Font de la Figuera
region_intro: Mountain biking around La Font De La Figuera offers varied terrain within the Costera comarca of Valencia, Spain. The region features a mountainous topography with an average altitude of 553 meters, characterized by prominent peaks such as El Capurutxo, Peña Foradada, and Alt de la Silla. Riders can expect routes that traverse dry farming landscapes, including vineyards, and areas within the Serra Grossa range. This diverse environment provides a range of challenges for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around La Font De La Figuera The most popular mountain bike trail is Cerro Capurutxo - Circular from La Font de la Figuera, a 37.3 miles (60.0 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route ascends El Capurutxo, the highest peak in the county, offering extensive panoramic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Restaurante Les Eres – Bosquet Reservoir loop from la Font de la Figuera, a difficult 34.4 miles (55.3 km) path. This route navigates varied terrain, including sections near the Bosquet Reservoir. Local mountain bikers also love the Restaurante Les Eres – Vallmelós Gorge loop from la Font de la Figuera, a 23.5 miles (37.8 km) trail leading through the Vallmelós Gorge, often completed in about 3 hours 22 minutes. Mountain biking around La Font De La Figuera is defined by mountainous topography, prominent peaks like Capurutxo, and dry farming landscapes with vineyards. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in La Font De La Figuera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Font De La Figuera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking around La Font de la Figuera?
A: The region offers a diverse and mountainous topography, with an average altitude of 553 meters. You'll encounter prominent peaks like El Capurutxo, Peña Foradada, and Alt de la Silla, providing challenging ascents and descents. The landscape also features dry farming areas, including picturesque vineyards, and sections within the Serra Grossa range, ensuring varied riding conditions.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in La Font de la Figuera?
A: Yes, while the area is known for its challenging routes, there are options for various skill levels. Out of 29 available mountain bike tours, 3 are rated as easy, providing a good starting point for less experienced riders or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking in the area?
A: You'll find several interesting natural features and landmarks. The highest peak, El Capurutxo, offers spectacular panoramic views. Other notable sites include the serene Balseta Pigirri with its spring, the forested area of El Regaixo, and the accessible first section of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6155567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cueva Santa&lt;/a&gt;. The region's dry farming landscape with vineyards also adds to the scenic charm.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around La Font de la Figuera are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e909657513/cerro-capurutxo-circular-from-la-font-de-la-figuera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Capurutxo - Circular from La Font de la Figuera&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that takes you to the highest peak in the county.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in La Font de la Figuera?
A: La Font de la Figuera is well-suited for outdoor activities, including mountain biking, for much of the year. The region's climate generally allows for pleasant riding conditions outside of the hottest summer months. Spring and autumn typically offer the most comfortable temperatures for exploring the mountainous terrain and vineyards.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes?
A: While many routes in the area are moderate to difficult, the 3 easy-rated tours can be suitable for families with some mountain biking experience. These trails offer a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes without the extreme challenges of the more advanced routes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in La Font de la Figuera?
A: The mountain bike trails in La Font de la Figuera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the challenging ascents to peaks like Capurutxo, and the scenic views across the dry farming landscapes and vineyards.

Q: Can I find routes that include the Vallmelós Gorge?
A: Yes, the Vallmelós Gorge is a prominent feature in some of the local mountain bike routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20131052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Restaurante Les Eres – Vallmelós Gorge loop from la Font de la Figuera&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 23.5-mile trail that takes you through this scenic gorge.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or water features?
A: Yes, you can find routes that incorporate local water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20131175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Restaurante Les Eres – Bosquet Reservoir loop from la Font de la Figuera&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 34.4-mile path that navigates varied terrain, including sections near the Bosquet Reservoir.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in La Font de la Figuera?
A: There are 29 mountain bike trails available around La Font de la Figuera, offering a range of difficulties from easy to difficult. This provides plenty of options for exploring the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What is the difficulty level of mountain biking trails in La Font de la Figuera?
A: The trails around La Font de la Figuera cater to various skill levels. Out of 29 routes, 3 are easy, 19 are moderate, and 7 are difficult. This means you can find everything from gentle rides through vineyards to challenging ascents up prominent peaks.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Santa Bárbara area?
A: Yes, the Sierra de la Oliva (or Sierra Santa Bárbara) is a significant feature in the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20131089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita Santa Bárbara – Vallmelós Gorge loop from la Font de la Figuera&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 43.8-kilometer route that includes this area, offering views towards the hermitage located on the summit.
tips: Beautiful views over the ravine and the track that runs alongside it is very entertaining, for the most part good road surface and very picturesque landscape.

At 940 meters above sea level, this site marks the limit between the Valencian Community and Castilla La Mancha. It is a place between groves and very quiet. It is accessed by the Navalón path by a track suitable for mtb or gravel.

Old site with 18th century buildings part of an old 19th century winery. The most recent construction in the form of a castle is a hotel project that has not yet been enabled. It is a very picturesque and charming place given the buildings and the tranquility of the place.

Place with incredible views that are worth the effort of having arrived there. It can be accessed by mtb or gravel and you can continue climbing to the forest hut or a few meters more on foot to seek more views.
